access.guest_fallback_message: Detailed explanations are available for logged-in Saola students only.

q27.explanation: Đoạn B tập trung vào ý chính được thể hiện qua câu/cụm thông tin: "Following the Napoleonic Wars in the early 19th century, there were many out-of-work naval officers, looking to find employment in the expanding British Empire. One of these was the naval surgeon John Richardson, born in Dumfries, Scotland, in 1787, who became a Fellow of the London Royal College of Surgeons in 1807, following a medical apprenticeship in Edinburgh.". Heading viii (Finding a man with the right skills for a voyage) bao quát nội dung của đoạn tốt hơn các heading còn lại.
q27.evidence: Following the Napoleonic Wars in the early 19th century, there were many out-of-work naval officers, looking to find employment in the expanding British Empire. One of these was the naval surgeon John Richardson, born in Dumfries, Scotland, in 1787, who became a Fellow of the London Royal College of Surgeons in 1807, following a medical apprenticeship in Edinburgh._

q28.explanation: Đoạn C tập trung vào ý chính được thể hiện qua câu/cụm thông tin: "At this time, the northern territories of Canada were dominated by two rival companies trading in animal furs and skins, who essentially governed this vast region. Staffed mainly by Scottish workers, but employing French voyageurs and Native American animal hunters, the violent trading war between these companies destabilised the whole of the region and the food stocks, which the expedition depended on, were simply unavailable.". Heading iv (Progress in the face of adversity) bao quát nội dung của đoạn tốt hơn các heading còn lại.
q28.evidence: At this time, the northern territories of Canada were dominated by two rival companies trading in animal furs and skins, who essentially governed this vast region. Staffed mainly by Scottish workers, but employing French voyageurs and Native American animal hunters, the violent trading war between these companies destabilised the whole of the region and the food stocks, which the expedition depended on, were simply unavailable._

q29.explanation: Đoạn D tập trung vào ý chính được thể hiện qua câu/cụm thông tin: "It was the difficulty of obtaining food which made them seriously consider ending the expedition. Travelling several hundred miles over the waterlogged bog and tundra, the hungry and exhausted travellers were forced to feed on the only plentiful plants they could find - primitive lichens covering otherwise bare rocks.". Heading x (The need to seek help) bao quát nội dung của đoạn tốt hơn các heading còn lại.
q29.evidence: It was the difficulty of obtaining food which made them seriously consider ending the expedition. Travelling several hundred miles over the waterlogged bog and tundra, the hungry and exhausted travellers were forced to feed on the only plentiful plants they could find - primitive lichens covering otherwise bare rocks._

q30.explanation: Đoạn E tập trung vào ý chính được thể hiện qua câu/cụm thông tin: "Meanwhile, Richardson had agreed to stay behind with two sick members of the party, Hood and Hepburn, near the Coppermine River. The three emaciated men survived on insects and sheltered in a small tent, but continued to observe morning and evening religious prayers to maintain morale.". Heading ix (Kill or be killed) bao quát nội dung của đoạn tốt hơn các heading còn lại.
q30.evidence: Meanwhile, Richardson had agreed to stay behind with two sick members of the party, Hood and Hepburn, near the Coppermine River. The three emaciated men survived on insects and sheltered in a small tent, but continued to observe morning and evening religious prayers to maintain morale._

q31.explanation: Đoạn F tập trung vào ý chính được thể hiện qua câu/cụm thông tin: "When Richardson and Hepburn later reached Fort Enterprise, they found the main party near to death from starvation and thoroughly demoralised, including their leader, Lieutenant Franklin. It was Richardson who then took charge, clearing out their filthy quarters and organising the sharing of what little sustenance they had, mainly consisting of old bones and scraps found outside.". Heading v (Coping as well as they could until help reached them) bao quát nội dung của đoạn tốt hơn các heading còn lại.
q31.evidence: When Richardson and Hepburn later reached Fort Enterprise, they found the main party near to death from starvation and thoroughly demoralised, including their leader, Lieutenant Franklin. It was Richardson who then took charge, clearing out their filthy quarters and organising the sharing of what little sustenance they had, mainly consisting of old bones and scraps found outside._

q32.explanation: Đoạn G tập trung vào ý chính được thể hiện qua câu/cụm thông tin: "Richardson later accompanied a second Franklin expedition in the years 1825-27, which was much better organised, enabling him to consolidate his collections and observations and forming the basis of the very first comprehensive study of the natural history of the Arctic. Following his knighthood in 1846, he led an unsuccessful search expedition to determine what had happened to John Franklin’s last Arctic expedition which had never returned and in which all died.". Heading ii (Returning to Canada) bao quát nội dung của đoạn tốt hơn các heading còn lại.
q32.evidence: Richardson later accompanied a second Franklin expedition in the years 1825-27, which was much better organised, enabling him to consolidate his collections and observations and forming the basis of the very first comprehensive study of the natural history of the Arctic. Following his knighthood in 1846, he led an unsuccessful search expedition to determine what had happened to John Franklin’s last Arctic expedition which had never returned and in which all died._
